Title: Genomic Analysis of Soybean PP2A-B'' Family and Its Effects on Drought and Salt Tolerance

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. GmPP2A-B''71 is a soybean PP2A-B'' regulatory subunit gene whose transcript is strongly induced by drought and salt; it shows high basal expression in roots and leaves.
2. GmPP2A-B''71 protein localizes to the nucleus and cytoplasm (GFP fusion in Arabidopsis protoplasts).
3. Overexpression of GmPP2A-B''71 enhances drought and salt tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis (Col-0) by increasing root length, fresh weight, survival rate, and reducing cell membrane damage (lower relative electrolyte leakage) under PEG and NaCl stress, with no penalty under normal conditions.
4. In soybean hairy root composite plants (cv. Williams 82), overexpression of GmPP2A-B''71 (OE) improves drought and salt tolerance, whereas RNAi knockdown increases sensitivity; OE lines show lower ROS accumulation (NBT staining), higher proline, chlorophyll, POD and CAT activities, and lower MDA than EV and RNAi lines under stress.
5. GmPP2A-B''71-OE soybean plants exhibit significantly higher expression of ROS-scavenging genes (GmCAT1, GmCAT2, GmPOD) and stress-related genes (GmLEA15, GmERF115), while these genes are down-regulated in GmPP2A-B''71-RNAi plants, indicating that GmPP2A-B''71 positively regulates the ROS scavenging and stress-response pathways to enhance drought and salt tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (transcriptome) of soybean under drought, salt, and full irrigation conditions; NCBI accession PRJNA694374.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of GmPP2A-B''71 enhances drought and salt tolerance in soybean and Arabidopsis, while RNAi knockdown in soybean increases sensitivity, largely through improved ROS scavenging and antioxidant capacity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a PP2A regulatory B'' subunit localized in nucleus and cytoplasm that positively regulates expression of ROS-scavenging enzymes (CAT, POD) and stress-related genes (LEA, ERF), leading to enhanced antioxidant enzyme activities, higher proline and chlorophyll, and reduced lipid peroxidation (MDA) under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ROS signaling and scavenging pathway involved in abiotic stress (drought and salt) tolerance; likely connected to broader stress/ABA-responsive transcriptional networks.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation:
GmPP2A-B''71 --(activation/upregulation)--> GmCAT1 (catalase gene; expression increased in OE and decreased in RNAi lines under drought and salt).
GmPP2A-B''71 --(activation/upregulation)--> GmCAT2.
GmPP2A-B''71 --(activation/upregulation)--> GmPOD (peroxidase gene).
GmPP2A-B''71 --(activation/upregulation)--> GmLEA15 (late embryogenesis abundant protein gene).
GmPP2A-B''71 --(activation/upregulation)--> GmERF115 (AP2/ERF transcription factor linked to ROS signaling).
These regulations are inferred from consistent qPCR upregulation in OE lines and downregulation in RNAi lines under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PP2A regulatory B'' subunit (PP2A-B''); member of PP2A-B'' family reported to contain EF-hand Ca2+-binding motifs.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm; determined by GmPP2A-B''71-GFP fusion transiently expressed in Arabidopsis leaf protoplasts (confocal microscopy).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern:
- Among 26 stress-responsive GmPP2A-B'' genes, GmPP2A-B''71 shows relatively high expression in soybean roots and leaves.
- Strongly induced by drought and salt in soybean (cv. Williams 82); transcript levels peak at 12 h of drought (250 mM mannitol) and 10 h of salt (200 mM NaCl) treatments.
- qPCR confirms strong induction under both drought and salt stresses compared with other nine tested GmPP2A-B'' genes.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor:
- Drought stress simulated by 250 mM mannitol for short-term expression assays in soybean and by PEG6000 (4% and 6%) and water withholding for 10–14 days in Arabidopsis and soybean composite plants.
- Salt stress applied as 200–250 mM NaCl in soybean (expression and tolerance assays) and 100–125 mM NaCl in Arabidopsis.
- All plants grown under controlled greenhouse/ growth-chamber conditions.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and salt) in soybean via PP2A-B''-mediated regulation of ROS signaling and scavenging.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance: drought tolerance; salt tolerance; oxidative stress (ROS) homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations:
- In Arabidopsis, GmPP2A-B''71-OE lines show significantly greater total root length and fresh weight than WT under 4–6% PEG and 100–125 mM NaCl, with no significant differences under normal conditions; survival rates are higher and relative electrolyte leakage is lower than WT under drought and salt stresses.
- In soybean hairy root composite plants, under drought and salt stress GmPP2A-B''71-OE plants have higher proline, chlorophyll, POD, and CAT levels and lower MDA than EV and RNAi plants; RNAi plants exhibit the opposite trend (all differences statistically significant at p < 0.05 or p < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects:
- GmPP2A-B''71-OE Arabidopsis plants maintain greener leaves and better growth under drought and salt; RNAi in soybean leads to severe leaf wilting and yellowing under stress.
- GmPP2A-B''71-OE soybean plants show reduced ROS accumulation in leaves and root tips (lighter NBT staining), while RNAi plants show stronger NBT staining, indicating higher ROS levels.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials:
- Soybean (Glycine max) variety 'Williams 82' for qPCR expression analysis and for creating hairy root composite plants (GmPP2A-B''71-OE, GmPP2A-B''71-RNAi, EV-control) via Agrobacterium rhizogenes K599.
- Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0 for stable overexpression of GmPP2A-B''71 (OE1, OE2, OE3) and for protoplast subcellular localization assays.
- Arabidopsis leaf protoplasts for transient GmPP2A-B''71-GFP localization.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods:
- Genome-wide identification and naming of 83 GmPP2A-B'' genes by BLAST search against the soybean genome (Phytozome), followed by chromosomal location, phylogenetic, gene structure, and motif analyses.
- RNA-seq analysis of soybean under drought, salt, and full irrigation to identify 26 stress-responsive GmPP2A-B'' genes; heat maps of expression in stresses and in nine tissues.
- Promoter cis-element analysis (1,500 bp upstream) of nine selected GmPP2A-B'' genes using PlantCARE.
- qPCR (2^-ΔΔCT method) to assess expression of nine GmPP2A-B'' genes under time-course drought (mannitol) and salt (NaCl) treatments, and expression of ROS-scavenging and stress-related genes (GmCAT1, GmCAT2, GmPOD, GmLEA15, GmERF115) in OE, RNAi, and EV plants.
- Subcellular localization by constructing GmPP2A-B''71-GFP fusion in 16318h-GFP vector and transient expression in Arabidopsis leaf protoplasts; confocal microscopy observation.
- Generation of GmPP2A-B''71-OE Arabidopsis lines via Agrobacterium tumefaciens-mediated transformation (pCAMBIA1302-GmPP2A-B''71), hygromycin selection, and analysis of T3/T4 lines.
- Generation of soybean hairy root composite plants via Agrobacterium rhizogenes K599 carrying GmPP2A-B''71-OE, GmPP2A-B''71-RNAi, or empty vector constructs.
- Drought and salt stress assays in Arabidopsis (PEG6000, NaCl, water withholding) and soybean composite plants (NaCl irrigation, water withholding) measuring phenotypes, survival rate, root length, fresh weight, and relative electrolyte leakage.
- Biochemical assays: NBT staining for ROS in leaves and root tips; measurement of proline, MDA, chlorophyll, CAT, and POD in soybean hairy roots and leaves using commercial kits and spectrophotometry.
- Statistical analysis by one-way ANOVA and t-tests (SPSS).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation via overexpression and RNAi knockdown in soybean hairy root composite plants, overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana, combined with subcellular localization (GFP fusion), physiological/biochemical assays (ROS, proline, MDA, chlorophyll, CAT, POD), and qPCR of ROS-scavenging and stress-related genes under drought and salt stress.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate gene identified from genome-wide PP2A-B'' family analysis and drought/salt-induced transcriptome data; GmPP2A-B''71 was selected based on strong stress inducibility and tissue expression, then cloned and functionally characterized by reverse genetics (overexpression and RNAi) in soybean and heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GmPP2A-B''71 is a strong candidate gene for improving soybean tolerance to drought and salt stress by enhancing ROS scavenging capacity and antioxidant defenses. Its overexpression confers higher survival, better growth, and reduced oxidative damage under stress without observable growth penalties under normal conditions, suggesting potential utility in transgenic or molecular breeding strategies to increase yield stability in saline and drought-prone environments.
